title: BSP CMD23 and auto-stop policy audit
observations: The BSP suppresses auto-stop when a request has SBC/CMD23, and may use an SoC-specific opacmd23 callback when provided.
Vendor A733 SDMMC0 does not set cap-cmd23; cap-cmd23 appears on the eMMC-style SDMMC2/SDMMC3 nodes.
The local failing proof logs CMD18 with auto-stop, but the failure is IDMAC stuck in descriptor read before descriptor word 0 is consumed.
Stop/CMD23 policy can affect transfer termination, but it does not name a memory-access precondition for fetching the first IDMAC descriptor.
reason: The BSP CMD23/auto-stop delta is not active for vendor SDMMC0 and is downstream of the observed descriptor-fetch stall.
next: Do not retest CMD23/autostop as a descriptor-fetch fix without new SDMMC0-specific evidence.
